delete_remote_node_desc: This removes the peer configuration. Any remote storage policies still bound to it must be reassigned first.
remote_node_enrollment_prepared: Remote node saved and enrollment command generated
remote_node_enrollment_completed_action_: This node has already completed enrollment and cannot generate another enrollment command
remote_node_overview_desc: Set the display name, transport mode, and optional outbound base URL for this peer.
remote_node_credentials_desc: These credentials are managed automatically for outbound signed requests to the peer. Manual edits are not supported in the current version; rotate will be added as a separate action later.
remote_node_routing_desc: Optional local policy used when the peer writes objects back into this node.
remote_node_enabled_desc: When disabled, the primary stops sending remote traffic to this node and the peer can no longer write objects into it.
remote_node_transport_direct_desc: Primary connects to the follower base URL directly.
remote_node_transport_reverse_tunnel_des: Follower keeps an outbound tunnel to primary; base URL can stay empty. This mode is still under test.
remote_node_transport_auto_desc: Uses direct transport only when a base URL is set; leaving it empty uses the reverse tunnel. It does not fail over automatically.
remote_node_base_url_hint: Optional for reverse tunnel. For direct transport, enter the follower URL that the primary can reach. Auto mode chooses direct only when this value is set; it does not retry through the tunnel if direct access fails.
remote_node_base_url_invalid: Enter a valid base URL that starts with http:// or https://.
remote_node_status_settings_desc: This switch only decides whether the primary should keep using this node. When disabled, the primary stops sending remote traffic to it and the peer can no longer push objects into this node. Which local policy receives those objects is still written on the follower during enroll, not here.
remote_node_diagnostics_desc: Saved capability probe details from the latest connection test.
remote_node_test_failed_confirm_desc: The current remote node settings failed the connection test. You can still save them, but remote storage traffic may fail until the peer configuration is fixed.
remote_node_wizard_step_identity_desc: Set the display name. Policies and signed requests will reference this record later.
remote_node_wizard_step_connection_desc: Choose whether the primary should connect directly or use the follower's outbound reverse tunnel. Managed credentials are generated when the record is saved.
remote_node_wizard_step_routing_desc: Choose which local policy should receive objects when the peer writes them back into this node.
remote_node_wizard_step_review_desc: Review the primary-to-follower enrollment flow. The button below saves the primary-side record first, then generates the follower-side enroll command.
remote_node_wizard_name_required: Enter a name for this remote node.
remote_node_name_hint: Use an environment- or region-based name so the node is easier to identify later.
remote_node_wizard_access_key_required: Access Key is required before creation.
remote_node_wizard_secret_key_required: Secret Key is required before creation.
remote_node_wizard_routing_helper_title: What does receive policy mean here?
remote_node_wizard_routing_helper_desc: When the peer writes objects back into this node, the selected local policy handles them first. Leaving it empty keeps the node record without assigning a default landing target.
remote_node_wizard_auto_credentials_desc: Access Key and Secret Key are managed by the system and generated on save, so they do not need to be entered manually. After save, they are bundled into the one-time enrollment command for the follower.
remote_node_wizard_followup_value: Generate the enroll command after saving the record
remote_node_wizard_docs_desc: If this follower will run in Docker, follow the bootstrap-ENV guide directly instead of manually entering the container to run enroll.
remote_node_primary_site_url_required: Set the primary public site URL in system settings before creating a remote node. The enrollment command needs a stable primary URL.
remote_node_enrollment_dialog_desc: The primary-side follower record has already been saved. Run the command below on the follower server to write the local master binding. Landing targets are configured separately from the primary UI.
remote_node_enrollment_flow_desc: You do not need both admin consoles open at the same time. Generate the command here, then run one enroll command on the follower server.
remote_node_enrollment_step_issue_desc: This command already includes the primary URL and enrollment token. Until the final ack succeeds and the session expires, the same command can be retried if something fails.
remote_node_enrollment_step_run_title: Run enroll on the follower server
remote_node_enrollment_step_run_desc: The CLI redeems bootstrap settings from the primary node, writes the local master binding on the follower, and flips runtime mode to follower.
remote_node_enrollment_step_restart_desc: This version writes configuration into the database, but the running service does not hot-reload remote nodes yet, so restart is still required.
remote_node_enrollment_command_desc: Copy this command to the follower server and run it there. If you later wrap this in a shell script, this can be the underlying command.
remote_node_enrollment_command_hint: Append `--database-url` only if the follower CLI must target a specific local database. Landing targets are now configured separately from this page on the primary side.
remote_node_enrollment_verify_desc: If you have already run enroll on the follower server and restarted the service, you can verify primary-to-follower connectivity right here.
remote_node_enrollment_verify_hint: A follower `base_url` is already configured, so this verification can run immediately. If it fails, continue checking the URL, certificate, or firewall configuration.
remote_node_enrollment_verify_disabled_h: No follower `base_url` is configured yet, so the primary cannot actively verify connectivity right now. Add the address first if you want to run the test.
ingress_policy_hint: Applied to objects accepted from this peer. Remote storage policies cannot be selected as ingress targets.
remote_node_ingress_profiles_desc: Primary decides where follower-side incoming objects land here. Local paths stay under the follower managed ingress root, multi-primary followers reject this mode, and remote writes stay blocked until a default landing target exists.
remote_node_ingress_profiles_base_url_re: Direct transport needs a reachable follower `base_url` before primary-side ingress profile management can proxy through the follower API.
remote_node_ingress_profiles_empty_desc: Create at least one local or S3 target and set a default, or this follower will refuse remote writes.
remote_node_ingress_profile_form_desc: Only local and S3 drivers are supported. The default profile becomes the landing target for managed follower ingress.
remote_node_ingress_profile_base_path_re: Base path is required for local ingress profiles.
Local base path must stay relative and cannot escape the managed ingress root.
remote_node_ingress_profile_endpoint_req: Endpoint is required for S3 ingress profiles.
remote_node_ingress_profile_bucket_requi: Bucket is required for S3 ingress profiles.
remote_node_ingress_profile_access_key_r: Access Key is required for S3 ingress profiles.
remote_node_ingress_profile_secret_key_r: Secret Key is required for S3 ingress profiles.
remote_node_ingress_profile_max_file_siz: Max file size must be a non-negative integer.
remote_node_ingress_profile_local_path_h: Relative only. The follower resolves this path under its managed ingress root.
remote_node_ingress_profile_s3_path_hint: Optional object prefix inside the bucket.
remote_node_ingress_profile_local_scope_: This profile writes into follower-local storage. The base path above is always resolved relative to `server.follower.managed_ingress_local_root`, never as an arbitrary host path.
remote_node_ingress_profile_default_togg: Use as the default ingress profile
remote_node_ingress_profile_default_hint: Managed follower ingress always lands on the default profile.
remote_node_ingress_profile_default_lock: This profile is already default. To change it, set another profile as default first.
remote_node_ingress_profile_credentials_: These credentials are required and will be stored on the follower.
Leave blank to keep the existing S3 credentials.
remote_node_ingress_profile_delete_desc: This removes the managed ingress target from the follower. Make another profile default first if this one is still the default and others remain.
